g.text: NOA Micro-TESE ML Prediction — Royan Institute Cohort (n = 2,413)

text.style: fill: #555555; font: italic 11px 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: middle

g.text: End-to-end data preprocessing and modeling pipeline
Source: Book_11.17.2025_Dr. Rad_Final ID.xlsb · 2,450 rows × 55 columns
Demographics · Hormones · Testicular · Pathology (free-text) · Genetics · Outcomes
9 cols 100% missing · 40 cols >50% · FSH 22% · LH 25% · Pathology-LT 49%
Cancer Type · Torsion · SHBG · TSH · Inhibin B · AMH · Infertility length · …
40 columns flagged; retained when clinically meaningful (Karyotype, Y-microdeletion)
Outcome1 = sperm retrieval (binary) · 37 rows missing → excluded · n = 2,413
Pipeline diverges: Paper 1 uses missingness indicators; Paper B uses kNN imputation
Regex: SCO · CSTH · MA stages 1–4 · Hypospermatogenesis · Bilateral (RT/LT)
16 pathology indicators + is_Primary + Salvage_Count · Encoding: ordinal_detailed_plus_raw
Mann-Whitney U (p<0.05) ∪ Mutual Information ∪ RFE-RF → 51 features (Paper 1) / 36 features (Paper B)
IQR method · Row integrity · Dtype coercion · MA hierarchy · Bilateral consistency
Median/IQR-based scaling · Fitted on training folds only · Robust to outliers
One-hot: Race, Habits, Karyotype, AZF · Binary: comorbidities, surgical history
Applied inside CV folds after split · Class ratio ~1.72:1 (failure:success)
n_train = 1,930 · n_test = 483 · Stratified on Outcome1 · random_state = 42
Top SHAP features: RT_Volume(Sono) · Sakamoto_RT · Infertile_Family · Seminal_pH · LT_Volume · Age

text.style: fill: #444444; font: italic 9.5px 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start

g.text: † Partner_Age excluded — biologically irrelevant to male sperm retrieval

text.style: fill: #555555; font: italic 9px 'DejaVu Sans'; text-anchor: start

g.text: (micro-TESE is a purely male-tissue surgical procedure; female partner age has no causal role)
